query: what is the weather in London right now
expected_use: MUST run duckduckgo-search-cli with -q -f json under GNU timeout 180 SIGTERM-first for dual+fetch default; MUST rely on default vertical all and content fetch ON with --fetch-content-cap default 10; MUST parse with jaq NEVER jq; MUST inspect .metadados.chrome_path_resolvido and .metadados.chrome_canal with values only manual|host|flatpak|snap NEVER env; MUST cite URLs; NEVER invent results; NEVER teach CHROME_PATH as product knob

query: Rust ecosystem news from the last week
expected_use: MUST use --vertical news with -q -f json; MUST parse .noticias[] including fonte and data_relativa via jaq; MUST treat Chrome-only fail-closed exit 2 without browser; content fetch remains ON by default for news cards under --fetch-content-cap unless --no-fetch-content

query: fast SERP-only lookup current USD BRL exchange rate headlines without page bodies
expected_use: MUST pass --no-fetch-content for the SERP-only fast path; MUST NOT treat --fetch-content as required; MUST use -q -f json and parse with jaq; default vertical remains dual all unless overridden

query: stream multi-query NDJSON for rust tokio and reqwest as each query completes
expected_use: MUST use multi-query --stream or -f ndjson under timeout; MUST parse one SearchOutput JSON line per query; MUST NOT treat stream as full SERP hit event stream; MUST know single-query --stream is ignored with warning; MUST use -q and jaq; MUST capture exit with PIPESTATUS when piping

query: duckduckgo-search-cli health check is Chrome ready and run doctor
expected_use: MUST run --probe and or doctor -q -f json; MUST inspect .status with jaq; MUST know Chrome-only production and exit 2 fail-closed when Chrome is missing; product has no NO_CHROME env kill-switch; MUST prefer doctor for environment diagnosis when asked for full health

query: deep research best Rust HTTP client comparison with multiple angles
expected_use: MUST run deep-research with dual web+news default under Chrome; MUST use --sub-query-strategy manual and --sub-queries-file; MUST aggregate and inspect .metadados.sub_queries plus .resultados[] and .noticias[]; MUST use -q -f json and jaq NEVER jq

query: set persistent proxy and show effective duckduckgo-search-cli config
expected_use: MUST use dual config API config set KEY VALUE or config set --key --value for proxy_url only from ALLOWED_KEYS; MUST run config effective; MUST NEVER invent config keys; MUST NEVER use HTTP_PROXY HTTPS_PROXY ALL_PROXY as product proxy; product config is CLI plus XDG only

query: batch search rust tokio chromiumoxide and reqwest from a queries file
expected_use: MUST use --queries-file with optional --parallel; MUST parse multi-query root .buscas[] NEVER confuse with single-query .resultados[]; MUST read .buscas[].metadados.chrome_path_resolvido and chrome_canal as manual|host|flatpak|snap; MUST use -q -f json and jaq

query: search failed with anti-bot exit 3 or 6 how do I recover
expected_use: On exit 3 or 6 MUST remediate with real Chrome --chrome-path and or --proxy via CLI or XDG and wait; MUST read ZeroCause .metadados.causa_zero and .metadados.sugestao_proxima_acao; NEVER use --allow-lite-fallback as remediation; NEVER Lite or HTTP downgrade; NEVER rely on HTTP_PROXY for product proxy

query: how should agents wrap duckduckgo-search-cli so Chromium cleans up and broken pipe is safe
expected_use: MUST treat each CLI invocation as process plus disk ONE-SHOT owner of Chromium Xvfb and ddg-chrome-* profile; MUST wrap with GNU timeout SIGTERM-first; MUST treat exit 141 broken pipe as non-search-failure; MUST know next run sweeps only stale owned ddg-chrome-*; NEVER bulk-rm foreign .tmp* or org.chromium.Chromium.*; NEVER expect auto cleanup after bare SIGKILL; product log is -q greater than -v or -vv greater than XDG log_directive greater than info NEVER RUST_LOG product config

query: fix the off-by-one bug in this local for-loop
expected_use: MUST NOT trigger duckduckgo-search-cli — pure local coding task with no need for live web sources

query: format this local JSON pretty-print indentation
expected_use: MUST NOT trigger web search — local formatting task

expected_use: MUST NOT trigger the CLI when internal knowledge suffices and the user did not ask for current external sources

expected_use: MUST NOT trigger duckduckgo-search-cli — local code generation without external sources

expected_use: MUST NOT trigger web search — pure math solvable locally

expected_use: MUST NOT trigger duckduckgo-search-cli — local refactor of provided code

expected_use: MUST NOT trigger web search — commit message from local diff only

expected_use: MUST NOT trigger the search CLI — local array processing
